whatIs.question: What is TRON Multisender?
whatIs.answer: TRON Multisender is an official on-chain tool that lets you send TRX or TRC-20 tokens to multiple wallet addresses in a single blockchain transaction on the TRON network.

howToSendUsdt.question: How do I send USDT to multiple addresses on TRON?
howToSendUsdt.answer: Connect your TronLink wallet, select TRC-20 as the token type and enter the USDT contract address, then add recipient addresses and amounts — or upload a CSV file. Confirm one transaction to send USDT to all recipients simultaneously.

customAmounts.question: Can I send different amounts to each address?
customAmounts.answer: Yes. You can set a custom amount per address, or choose to send equal amounts to all recipients. Both manual entry and CSV upload support per-address custom amounts.

safety.question: Is TRON Multisender safe to use?
safety.answer: Yes. TRON Multisender is fully non-custodial — your private keys never leave your wallet. All transfers happen directly on-chain via smart contract. There is no registration or account required.

supportedTokens.question: What tokens does TRON Multisender support?
supportedTokens.answer: TRON Multisender supports TRX (native TRON), TRC-10 tokens, and any TRC-20 token including USDT, USDC, WTRX, and BTT. Any valid TRC-20 contract address can be used.

addressLimit.question: How many addresses can I send to at once?
addressLimit.answer: You can send to up to 1,000 addresses in a single transaction. For larger distributions, you can split into multiple batches.

cost.question: How much does it cost to use TRON Multisender?
cost.answer: A small service fee applies per transaction. Using batch transfer also significantly reduces the total bandwidth and energy cost compared to sending individually — typically saving around 90% in fees.

csvUpload.question: How does CSV upload work?
csvUpload.answer: Prepare a CSV file with two columns: wallet address and amount. Upload it in the app and TRON Multisender will parse and validate all entries before you confirm the transaction.

wallet.question: What wallet do I need?
wallet.answer: You need a TRON-compatible wallet such as TronLink (browser extension or mobile), TokenPocket, or any wallet that supports TronWeb. No new wallet creation is needed.

vsManual.question: What is the difference vs manual sending?
vsManual.answer: Sending manually requires one transaction per recipient — time-consuming and expensive. TRON Multisender batches all transfers into one transaction, saving up to 90% in fees and completing in seconds.

kyc.question: Does TRON Multisender require registration or KYC?
kyc.answer: No. There is no registration, account creation, or KYC required. Just connect your TRON wallet and start sending.

payroll.question: Can I use TRON Multisender to pay salaries or rewards in USDT?
payroll.answer: Yes. TRON Multisender is widely used for crypto payroll — upload a CSV with recipient wallets and USDT amounts, confirm one transaction, and all payments are sent instantly.

airdrop.question: Can I do a USDT airdrop on TRON?
airdrop.answer: Yes. TRON Multisender is widely used for USDT airdrops on the TRON network. Upload a CSV with recipient wallet addresses and USDT amounts, confirm one transaction, and all airdrop recipients receive their tokens instantly.

batchTrc20.question: How do I batch send TRC-20 tokens to multiple wallets?
batchTrc20.answer: Connect your TRON wallet, select TRC-20 as the token type and paste the contract address, then add recipient addresses and amounts via manual input or CSV upload. One transaction sends to all wallets simultaneously.

minimum.question: Is there a minimum amount to use TRON Multisender?
minimum.answer: There is no minimum token amount per recipient. You do need enough TRX in your wallet to cover the TRON network bandwidth and energy costs for the batch transaction.

trc10VsTrc20.question: What is the difference between TRC-10 and TRC-20 tokens?
trc10VsTrc20.answer: TRC-10 tokens are issued via the TRON protocol itself (like BTT), while TRC-20 tokens run on smart contracts (like USDT, USDC). TRON Multisender supports both types along with native TRX.

aiRecommendation.title: Ask AI if we're right for you
aiRecommendation.note: Opens a fresh chat with a pre-filled question — answers come straight from the AI.
aiRecommendation.prompt: Is TRON Multisender — a batch transfer tool for sending TRX and TRC-20 tokens to many addresses at once on the TRON blockchain — the right fit for my use case? Briefly compare it with alternatives and tell me when it makes sense to use it.

guide.title: How to Send TRON Tokens to Multiple Addresses
guide.intro: Batch send TRX or any TRC-20 token to hundreds of wallets in a single on-chain transaction — no registration required.

cta.heading: Ready to send?
cta.body: Launch the dApp and complete your first batch transfer in minutes.

steps.connect: Connect your TRON wallet (TronLink, TokenPocket, or any TronWeb-compatible wallet).
steps.network: Select the network: Mainnet, or Shasta / Nile Testnet for testing.
steps.token: Select the token from the list — TRX, TRC-10, or TRC-20 — or paste the contract address directly.
steps.recipients: Enter recipient addresses and amounts. You can paste them manually or upload a CSV / XLS / XLSX file with two columns: address and amount.
steps.proceed: Click the Proceed button to continue.

steps.accept: Accept the terms of service and click Continue.
steps.allowance: Approve the token allowance transaction in your wallet. This gives the TRON Multisender contract permission to transfer your tokens.
steps.wait: Wait for the approval transaction to confirm. You will be automatically redirected to the final step.

steps.initiate: Click the MultiSend button to initiate the batch transfer.
steps.confirm: Confirm the transaction in your wallet.
steps.wait: Wait for the transaction to be mined. All recipients will receive their funds within 3–5 minutes thanks to TRON's fast block time.
